pattern_candidates.name: scrapeURL engine-fallback chain
pattern_candidates.description: Pluggable extraction engines coordinated by a fallback-list builder. buildFallbackList → scrapeURLWithEngine → (success?) → try next or NoEnginesLeftError. Named failure mode, documented order, engine-level telemetry.

pattern_candidates.name: Capability-gated test pattern
pattern_candidates.description: Gate tests by runtime capability (env vars) not by CI fence. Same test file runs in all environments; gate decides whether assertions execute. Three lines per gate.

pattern_candidates.name: Harness-as-boot-orchestrator
pattern_candidates.description: Single harness.ts script owns service boot order (API + workers + emulator) before test runner. CLAUDE.md mandates it; no ad-hoc `pnpm start` in dev docs.

pattern_candidates.name: Version-parallel controller trees
pattern_candidates.description: Parallel v0/v1/v2 controller directories with their own test fixtures. New features land on v2; v0 stays alive for compat. Three OpenAPI specs frozen at repo root.

pattern_candidates.name: Redlock distributed cache serialization
pattern_candidates.description: Use redlock.using([key], ttlMs, ...) to serialise cache updates across multiple pods. Prevents race conditions on shared cache slots (e.g. ACUC credit updates).

knowledge_candidates.name: Multi-agent orchestration via openai_swarm
knowledge_candidates.description: Reference implementation of multi-agent coordination around a single scraping task. Worth reading to inform SoNash agent teams (.claude/teams/) and the agent boundary design work for JASON-OS.

knowledge_candidates.name: Deep Research API usage pattern
knowledge_candidates.description: How firecrawl's Deep Research API is invoked in practice (apartment-finder example). Interactive preference gathering → research call → LLM analysis → structured JSON. Compare to SoNash /deep-research skill's searcher pipeline.

knowledge_candidates.name: GitHub repo analysis via firecrawl Map API
knowledge_candidates.description: examples/gemini-github-analyzer uses firecrawl's Map API to walk a GitHub repo. Direct domain overlap with SoNash's /repo-analysis — worth comparing extraction approaches.

knowledge_candidates.name: Docs-to-OpenAPI extraction
knowledge_candidates.description: examples/turning_docs_into_api_specs — convert a documentation site into an OpenAPI spec via scraping. Informs SoNash document-analysis handler — a docs site IS a kind of structured document.

knowledge_candidates.name: Commercial-OSS feature tiering via .env
knowledge_candidates.description: Fire-engine is hosted-only; self-hosted users get documented limited CAPTCHA handling. Pattern: tier features by backing service availability, document the split explicitly.

content_candidates.name: scrapeURL engine-fallback README
content_candidates.description: Mermaid signal flow diagram for the engine fallback chain. The whole extraction thesis in four nodes. Worth re-reading when expanding SoNash website-analysis.

content_candidates.name: 58-example cookbook pattern
content_candidates.description: The examples/ directory as product-marketing-in-code. 58 self-contained apps, each pairing firecrawl with a specific LLM or framework. Stealable pattern for SoNash skill documentation.

content_candidates.name: openai_swarm_firecrawl example
content_candidates.description: Concrete multi-agent coordination code. Direct reference for SoNash's agent team boundary design.

content_candidates.name: gemini-github-analyzer example
content_candidates.description: Repo-walking via firecrawl Map API, same domain as SoNash repo-analysis.

content_candidates.name: deep-research-apartment-finder example
content_candidates.description: Deep Research API usage pattern from a Python CLI.

content_candidates.name: turning_docs_into_api_specs example
content_candidates.description: Docs-to-OpenAPI extraction pattern. Informs SoNash document-analysis.

content_candidates.name: CLAUDE.md minimal-actionable exemplar
content_candidates.description: 19-line CLAUDE.md that beats most 200-line variants. Test-first, E2E preferred, capability-gating, harness usage. Worth a direct comparison against SoNash's 235-line CLAUDE.md for signal density.

anti_pattern_candidates.name: Zero architecture prose docs
anti_pattern_candidates.description: 1162 files, one mermaid diagram, no docs/ directory. Queue layout, auth flow, service boundaries all have to be inferred from code. New contributors pay the ramp-up tax. SoNash should file this as evidence in the next 'do we need this ADR?' conversation.

anti_pattern_candidates.name: Two queue systems coexisting without migration plan
anti_pattern_candidates.description: BullMQ (Redis) and nuq-postgres (custom Postgres queue) both active in services/. Rationale undocumented. Suggests in-progress migration with no exit criteria. If SoNash ever needs a queue, pick one and commit — don't run two.

anti_pattern_candidates.name: Hand-rolled polyglot SDK parity
anti_pattern_candidates.description: Seven SDKs (JS, Python, Rust, Go, Java, Elixir, playwright-service) re-implement the wire contract manually. Parity enforced by OpenAPI specs + test-suite app. Visible tax: adding an endpoint is a 7-file change. Correct answer for SoNash if ever pressured: generate from OpenAPI or say no.

anti_pattern_candidates.name: Hidden feature flags via env vars
anti_pattern_candidates.description: Firecrawl branches behaviour through SELF_HOST.md env vars (USE_DB_AUTHENTICATION, PROXY_SERVER, SEARXNG_*) with no documented feature-flag layer. Fine at small-team scale, breaks down as team grows. Prefer explicit feature flags over env-var branching for user-visible behaviour.

related_repos.detail: Python-side alternative to firecrawl's TS scraping. Wave 4 quick-scan #3. Worth comparing API design and engine abstraction approaches once crawl4ai is at Standard depth.
PDF → Markdown pipeline. Firecrawl uses LlamaParse for PDFs; marker is an alternative engine. Wave 4 quick-scan #4.
OCR/layout companion to marker. Part of the same PDF-extraction pipeline family. Wave 4 quick-scan #5.
LLM-first reader proxy. Alternative architecture for 'clean web data for LLMs'. Wave 4 quick-scan #6.

cross_repo_connections.detail: Both are 'make the web LLM-consumable' frameworks. Firecrawl is TypeScript-first with a commercial tier; crawl4ai is Python-first. Engine abstraction approaches may differ — worth comparing once crawl4ai is at Standard depth.
Firecrawl uses LlamaParse for PDF→Markdown. Marker is an alternative extraction engine in the same domain. The PDF pipeline comparison is a Wave 5 synthesis theme.
Gemini-based GitHub repo analyzer inside this repo. Direct overlap with SoNash repo-analysis skill. Future investigation: compare extraction approaches and prompting.
